page.primary_keyword: Marbella family boat day paddleboarding snorkeling
page.title: Marbella Family Boat Day: Paddleboarding and Snorkeling Adventure

data.meta_description: Book a Marbella family boat day with paddleboarding and snorkeling from Puerto Banús. 12.5m Astondoa 40 from €749/2h. Licensed skipper, drinks, snacks included.
data.summary: A family-friendly boat charter from Puerto Banús combining paddleboarding and snorkeling along the Marbella coast. Our 12.5m Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 offers space for up to 11 guests with all gear included.
key_facts: 12.5m Astondoa 40 (9 pax) or Azimut 39 (11 pax) from Puerto Banús
From €749 for 2 hours up to €2,299 for 8 hours (all inclusive)
Paddleboard and snorkel gear provided on board
Licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, and IVA included
Soft drinks, beer, cava, white wine, water, and light snacks included
Family-friendly itinerary: Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, or Cabopino coves
Departure and return from Puerto Banús, Marbella

faq.q: What boats are available for a family boat day in Marbella?
faq.a: Our family-friendly fleet includes the 12.5m Astondoa 40 (9 pax) and the 12.5m Azimut 39 (11 pax), both departing from Puerto Banús. For larger groups, the 17.6m Azimut 58 Flybridge or 24m Mangusta 80 can also be arranged – pricing on request.

faq.q: Is paddleboarding and snorkeling gear included in the charter?
faq.a: Yes. Every family charter includes paddleboards and snorkeling masks, fins, and snorkels. Our skipper will recommend the best spots for calm paddling and clear snorkeling, such as Cala del Faro or the coves near Cabopino.

faq.q: What is included in the price for a Marbella family boat day?
faq.a: The price covers a licensed skipper, fuel, paddleboards, snorkeling gear, water, soft drinks, beer, cava, white wine, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden extras.

faq.q: How long should we charter the boat for a family day with paddleboarding and snorkeling?
faq.a: A 4-hour charter is ideal for a relaxed morning or afternoon, allowing time to cruise to a cove, paddleboard, snorkel, and enjoy snacks. An 8-hour day gives you time for lunch afloat, multiple stops, and a longer swim.

faq.q: Can we bring our own food and drinks on board?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and drinks. Our standard package already includes soft drinks, beer, cava, white wine, water, and light snacks. If you want a catered lunch, let us know and we can arrange it at an extra cost.

faq.q: What is the best time of year for a family boat day in Marbella?
faq.a: May through October offers the warmest sea temperatures (20–26°C) and calmest conditions for paddleboarding and snorkeling. July and August are peak season with long sunny days, but spring and autumn provide quieter coves.

faq.q: Do we need to know how to paddleboard or snorkel?
faq.a: No experience is necessary. Our skipper will give a quick safety briefing and basic instructions. Paddleboards are stable and suitable for children aged 6+. Snorkeling gear is simple to use, and we can recommend shallow, sheltered coves.

faq.q: How do we book a Marbella family boat day with paddleboarding and snorkeling?
faq.a: Contact us via WhatsApp on the number shown on our site. We will confirm availability for your date, suggest the best boat for your group size, and send a booking link. A 50% deposit secures the charter, with the balance due on the day.

data.body_html:
A Marbella family boat day with paddleboarding and snorkeling is one of the best ways to spend quality time on the Costa del Sol. Departing from Puerto Banús, your private charter gives you a licensed skipper, all safety gear, and a stable platform for paddling and snorkelling in the calm Mediterranean coves. Whether you choose the 12.5m Astondoa 40 (9 pax) or the 12.5m Azimut 39 (11 pax), the experience is designed for families with children aged 6 and up.

Why choose a family boat day in Marbella?

Marbella’s coastline offers sheltered coves, clear water, and reliable summer weather. A private boat means you avoid crowded beaches and set your own pace. Paddleboarding and snorkelling are included at no extra cost, and our skipper knows the best spots for each activity. The whole family can swim, paddle, and explore without worrying about logistics. From €749 for a 2-hour trip to €2,299 for a full 8-hour day, the price includes everything except a catered lunch (which we can arrange on request).

Our family-friendly boats

We offer two ideal boats for a family day out. Both depart from Puerto Banús and include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and IVA.

  • Astondoa 40 – 12.5m, up to 9 guests. A spacious flybridge with a large sundeck and a bathing platform for easy water access.
  • Azimut 39 – 12.5m, up to 11 guests. A sporty cruiser with a generous cockpit and a swim ladder.

For larger families or groups, enquire about our Marbella boat rental fleet, which includes the 17.6m Azimut 58 Flybridge (12 pax) and 24m Mangusta 80 (12 pax) – pricing on request.

Paddleboarding and snorkelling included

Every family charter comes with paddleboards and snorkelling gear (masks, fins, snorkels). Our skipper will recommend the best spots based on sea conditions and your family’s experience level.

  • Cala del Faro – a rocky cove with clear water, ideal for snorkelling around the rocks.
  • Cala Cortés – a sandy beach reachable only by boat, perfect for paddleboarding.
  • Cabopino – a series of small coves with calm water and sandy seabeds.

Paddleboards are stable and suitable for children aged 6 and up. Life jackets are provided for all ages.

Sample itinerary for a 4-hour family boat day

A typical 4-hour morning or afternoon trip might look like this:

  • 10:00 – Board your boat at Puerto Banús. Safety briefing from the skipper.
  • 10:15 – Cruise east along the coast past the Golden Mile and Marbella Marina.
  • 10:45 – Arrive at Cala del Faro. Anchor and start paddleboarding and snorkelling.
  • 12:00 – Enjoy soft drinks, beer, cava, and light snacks on board.
  • 12:30 – Cruise back to Puerto Banús, passing La Concha mountain.
  • 13:00 – Disembark.

For an 8-hour day, add a stop for lunch (bring your own or order a catering hamper) and a second cove such as Cabopino or Estepona.

What to bring for a family boat day

  • Swimwear, towels, and sunscreen (SPF 50+ recommended)
  • Rash vests or wetsuits for children (sea temp 18–26°C depending on season)
  • Sunglasses and a hat
  • Waterproof phone pouch or camera
  • Your own food if you want more than the included snacks

Our boat is equipped with a shaded cockpit, a sundeck, and a small fridge for your drinks.

Booking your Marbella family boat day

To book, simply contact us via WhatsApp. We will confirm availability, suggest the best boat for your group size, and send a booking link. A 50% deposit secures the date, with the balance due on the day. We recommend booking at least 48 hours in advance, especially during July and August.

Explore all our Marbella boat rental options or check our experiences for more ideas like sunset cruises or dolphin-watching trips.

Frequently asked questions

What boats are available for a family boat day in Marbella?

Our family-friendly fleet includes the 12.5m Astondoa 40 (9 pax) and the 12.5m Azimut 39 (11 pax), both departing from Puerto Banús. For larger groups, the 17.6m Azimut 58 Flybridge or 24m Mangusta 80 can also be arranged – pricing on request.

Is paddleboarding and snorkeling gear included in the charter?

Yes. Every family charter includes paddleboards and snorkeling masks, fins, and snorkels. Our skipper will recommend the best spots for calm paddling and clear snorkeling, such as Cala del Faro or the coves near Cabopino.

What is included in the price for a Marbella family boat day?

The price covers a licensed skipper, fuel, paddleboards, snorkeling gear, water, soft drinks, beer, cava, white wine, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden extras.

How long should we charter the boat for a family day with paddleboarding and snorkeling?

A 4-hour charter is ideal for a relaxed morning or afternoon, allowing time to cruise to a cove, paddleboard, snorkel, and enjoy snacks. An 8-hour day gives you time for lunch afloat, multiple stops, and a longer swim.

Can we bring our own food and drinks on board?

Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and drinks. Our standard package already includes soft drinks, beer, cava, white wine, water, and light snacks. If you want a catered lunch, let us know and we can arrange it at an extra cost.

What is the best time of year for a family boat day in Marbella?

May through October offers the warmest sea temperatures (20–26°C) and calmest conditions for paddleboarding and snorkeling. July and August are peak season with long sunny days, but spring and autumn provide quieter coves.

Do we need to know how to paddleboard or snorkel?

No experience is necessary. Our skipper will give a quick safety briefing and basic instructions. Paddleboards are stable and suitable for children aged 6+. Snorkeling gear is simple to use, and we can recommend shallow, sheltered coves.

How do we book a Marbella family boat day with paddleboarding and snorkeling?

Contact us via WhatsApp on the number shown on our site. We will confirm availability for your date, suggest the best boat for your group size, and send a booking link. A 50% deposit secures the charter, with the balance due on the day.
